Tushar Lab

Research

We develop physics-based simulation and AI frameworks to transform how medical imaging systems are evaluated and optimized.

🫁

01

Virtual Imaging Trials

We develop end-to-end virtual imaging trial (VIT) frameworks that simulate the patient, imaging system, and image reader computationally. Our flagship project, the Virtual Lung Screening Trial (VLST), enables large-scale evaluation of CT protocols without patient exposure.

Active Projects

  • Virtual Lung Screening Trial (VLST)
  • SYN-LUNGS
  • Digital Human Phantoms
🧠

02

Medical Imaging AI

We build foundation models and generative AI pipelines that address chronic data scarcity in medical imaging. Our NodMAISI and DukeSeg projects push the frontier of lesion detection, segmentation, and classification with minimal labeled data.

Active Projects

  • NodMAISI
  • DukeSeg
  • Foundation Models for CT
📊

03

Image Quality & Evaluation

We develop novel metrics and computational evaluation tools — including the CPU-Index framework — that quantify CT image quality in clinically meaningful, task-based terms. Our work connects physics-based quality assessment with downstream AI performance.

Active Projects

  • CPU-Index Framework
  • Task-Based Image Quality
  • AI Model Benchmarking
⚙️

04

Generative AI for Imaging

Leveraging diffusion models and physics-guided generative approaches, we synthesize realistic medical images with controllable pathology, anatomy, and acquisition parameters. This enables large-scale training of AI models without compromising patient privacy.

Active Projects

  • SYN-LUNGS Synthesis
  • Pathology-Conditioned CT Generation
  • Privacy-Preserving Data Augmentation

Collaboration

Interested in collaborating?

We welcome collaborations with clinical, industry, and academic partners. Reach out to discuss potential projects.
